summary: Matched the Dr. Mario pill item source file, described by the author as their first 100% match. The PR added the Dr. Mario pill ItemStateTable, implemented several spawn/setup entry points, adjusted function prototypes in the header, and refactored local code shape with helper macros, inline helpers, and no-inline wrapper chains to satisfy matching constraints. Review discussion specifically called out the wrapper-chain approach as suspicious and suggested that a cleaner future solution would be to find the original call chain, possibly through Mario fireball or related item code.
what_changed: Added forward declarations for Dr. Mario pill item state callbacks and defined ItemStateTable it_803F75D0 with seven state entries using callbacks such as it_802C0C0C, it_802C0C68, it_802C0CC4, it_802C0E48, it_802C1114, it_802C1148, it_802C1234, it_802C1340, and it_802C1360.
Added includes for baselib/gobj.h and db/db.h to support GObj helpers and debug/linking calls such as db_80225DD8.

Reworked it_802C061C to use GET_ITEM, local Vec3 storage, ftLib_80086644, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, and HSD_JObjSetRotationZ. The diff changed the final rotation reset from SetRotationY to SetRotationZ.
Introduced copy_jobj_scale as an inline helper that copies source JObj scale Y into all three scale components before HSD_JObjSetScale.
Reworked it_802C1234 to preserve the original item GObj, use GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ, test the linked fighter GObj through ftCo_800BF228, and copy fighter JObj scale onto the pill item when appropriate.

Implemented it_802C09C4 as a Dr. Mario pill spawn/setup routine taking Fighter_GObj parent_gobj, Vec3 position, arg2, ItemKind, msid, Fighter_Part, and facing_dir. It initializes SpawnItem, creates the item with Item_80268B18, changes state via it_802C1180 wrapper calls, sets animation/update state, clears item command variables, assigns accessory callback and owner fields, stores parent and facing direction in drmariopill item vars, and optionally calls Item_8026AB54 when msid == 2.
Refactored it_802C0CC4 to use GET_ITEM and GET_ATTRS and compare horizontal velocity magnitude from my_sqrtf against attrs->x10 before playing Item_8026AE84.
Refactored it_802C0B5C to use GET_ITEM and GET_ATTRS and compute initial pill velocity from attrs x0/x4, facing_dir, cosf, and sinf.

Changed it_802C0E48 from s32 to bool and split child-JObj visibility flag handling into inline helper it_802C0E48_flags. The function now uses GET_JOBJ/GET_ITEM, ftDr_Init_80149844, ftDr_Init_801498EC, ftLib_800869D4, and it_802C0E48_sub to handle attached/owned pill behavior.

Added it_802C0510 and it_802C0850 spawn/setup functions with shared SpawnItem initialization patterns, item creation via Item_80268B18, state/setup calls, debug attachment via db_80225DD8 in relevant paths, accessory callback assignment, owner/item-var initialization, and physics/update calls.
Added several static no-inline wrapper functions around it_802C0B5C, it_802C0DF8, and it_802C1180 to force code generation/call shape needed for matching.
Updated itdrmariopill.h prototype for it_802C09C4 from loosely typed enum_t/bool/bool parameters to s32 arg2, ItemKind arg3, s32 arg4, Fighter_Part arg5, and f32 facing_dir.
smart_moves: Used GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, and GET_ATTRS macros to reduce raw user_data and article-data casts while preserving matching-sensitive code shape.
Factored repeated scale-copy logic into a small inline helper, matching the observed all-components-from-scaleY behavior without duplicating assignment noise.
Separated it_802C0E48 child-JObj flag toggling into it_802C0E48_flags, making a matching-sensitive function easier to reason about while preserving the exact ClearFlags/SetFlags behavior.
Corrected the public header signature for it_802C09C4 to expose concrete item/fighter types, improving call-site type information for future matching work.
Kept the original custom my_sqrtf note that this file needs 4 iterations instead of the usual 3, preserving an important matching-specific math detail.
decomp_lessons: For item spawn routines, matching often depends on exact SpawnItem initialization order: kind, prev_pos with z forced to 0, it_8026BB68-derived spawn.pos, facing_dir, damage, zero velocity, parent pointers, flag b0, x40, and then Item_80268B18.

Accessory-linked items may need both owner fields and item-specific vars initialized consistently: owner, xDD4_itemVar.drmariopill.x4 parent pointer, xDD4_itemVar.drmariopill.x0 facing direction, on_accessory callback, and xDC8 flags.
Small helper extraction can improve readability without necessarily breaking matching, but only if inlining/no-inlining behavior is controlled deliberately.
No-inline wrapper chains can be a temporary matching tactic, but reviewers considered them a sign that the real original call chain may not yet be identified.
Header prototypes should be tightened once evidence supports concrete parameter types; replacing bool/enum placeholders with ItemKind, Fighter_Part, s32, and f32 makes later decomp work safer.
When an item mirrors fighter scale, the observed behavior here copied HSD_JObjGetScaleY from the fighter JObj into x, y, and z of the item scale vector.
naming_conventions: File-local matching helpers used address-based names plus suffixes such as _no_inline, _no_inline2, and _no_inline3 when their purpose was codegen control rather than semantic naming.
The item state table retained the address-style symbol name it_803F75D0.
The attributes macro is named GET_ATTRS and casts ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itDrMarioPillAttributes*.
Function parameters in the updated header use project types ItemKind, Fighter_Part, Fighter_GObj, Item_GObj, Vec3, s32, and f32 rather than generic bool/int placeholders.
Ambiguous parameters in it_802C09C4 were left as arg2, arg3, arg4, and arg5 where semantics were not fully established, avoiding invented names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used chained no-inline wrappers around it_802C1180, it_802C0DF8, and it_802C0B5C to force particular call structure for matching.
Used static inline helpers such as copy_jobj_scale and it_802C0E48_flags to control emitted code shape while grouping repeated logic.
Preserved a custom my_sqrtf implementation noted as requiring 4 iterations instead of the usual 3.
Introduced PAD_STACK(4) in it_802C0E48 after refactoring, indicating stack-shape tuning was still relevant.
Changed some local declarations and assignment grouping, such as scale.x = scale.y = scale.z = mag, to fit expected generated code.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.comment: surely there is a less insane way to do this...?
review_feedback.context: Commented on added no-inline wrapper chains around pill setup/state functions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Yeah, by finding the original call chain. Maybe it's calling something from Mario fireball or elsewhere, which is calling something else.
review_feedback.context: Suggested that the wrapper chains might be masking a missing original call chain, possibly through Mario fireball or related code.

follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs touching itdrmariopill.c to see whether the no-inline wrapper chains were replaced by the real original call chain.
Compare Dr. Mario pill spawn/setup routines against Mario fireball item code for shared helper calls or original call-chain evidence.
Audit call sites of it_802C09C4 after the header signature change to confirm parameter meanings for arg2, msid/arg4, and Fighter_Part.
Look for matching notes around my_sqrtf 4-iteration behavior in other item files.
